generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance VehicleSteeringServo : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Manages steering mechanisms in the physical vehicle system via ROS2 communication for distributed coordination"
]

instance MotorSpeedController : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Controls motor speed for vehicle actuation through Vedder Electronic Speed Controller implementation"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARUST10LX : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR providing 2D point cloud data for spatial mapping and obstacle detection with 0.4° angular resolution and 10m range"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera generating 3D point clouds and depth maps with 1080p resolution and sub-millimeter precision"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraPointCloudData
]

instance VehicleSpeedSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Structured telemetry stream capturing vehicle speed from motor subsystem"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleSpeedTelemetry
]

instance SteeringAngleSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Structured telemetry stream capturing steering angle from steering subsystem"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringAngleTelemetry
]

instance MotorActuatorStatus : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Structured telemetry stream capturing motor actuation status"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorActuationStatus
]

instance ServoStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Structured telemetry stream capturing servo status from steering system"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ServoStatusTelemetry
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction - Data Transmitted
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARUST10LX
    DTDFVocab:producedAt ROS2Network
]

instance DepthCameraPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDepthCamera
    DTDFVocab:producedAt ROS2Network
]

instance VehicleSpeedTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleSpeedSensor
    DTDFVocab:producedAt ROS2Network
]

instance SteeringAngleTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringAngleSensor
    DTDFVocab:producedAt ROS2Network
]

instance MotorActuationStatus : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuatorStatus
    DTDFVocab:producedAt ROS2Network
]

instance ServoStatusTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ServoStatusSensor
    DTDFVocab:producedAt ROS2Network
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance VehicleStateModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model capturing vehicle operational dynamics including speed, steering, and motor control logic"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationComponent
]

instance EnvironmentalMappingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric model representing spatial environment captured from LiDAR and depth camera data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringComponent
]

instance ProcessedObjectTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data-driven model for clustering and tracking objects from raw point cloud data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringComponent
]

instance DataCollectionDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured storage of processed vehicle states externally for data integrity verification"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleSpeedTelemetry
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataStorageGenerationFE
]

instance ObjectVisualizationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed object tracks and environmental maps for visualization and reporting"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData, DepthCameraPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PresentationFE
]

instance RealTimeDashboards : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time performance metrics and KPIs for user cockpit visualization"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleSpeedTelemetry, SteeringAngleTelemetry
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PresentationFE
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2Network : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Standardized ROS2 communication network ensuring message serialization, type alignment, and unit conversion"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTranslationComponent
]

instance WiFiNetwork : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi transport layer for inter-entity communication (implied UDP usage) between vehicle and external systems"
]

instance DataTranslationComponent : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component performing message type alignment and unit conversions between different sensor units"
]

instance PointCloudClusteringComponent : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component handling raw LiDAR point cloud normalization, clustering, and filtering"
]

instance DataPreProcessingFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Feature Engine processing raw LiDAR point clouds for environmental mapping and object tracking"
]

instance DataCollectionFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Feature Engine aggregating processed data for off-board analysis and reporting"
]

instance PresentationFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Feature Engine formatting visualizations for user cockpit and 3D/4D modeling"
]

instance DataStorageGenerationFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Feature Engine ensuring data integrity by storing processed vehicle states externally"
]

instance UserInterfaceFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Feature Engine providing interactive controls and visualization interfaces for users"
]

instance 3DVisualizationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Engine enabling interactive 3D modeling of physical systems for monitoring and diagnostics"
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous real-time observation of vehicle performance states including speed, diagnostics, and battery status"
    DTDFVocab:provides VehiclePerformanceInsights
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic optimization of operational parameters for fuel efficiency and resource allocation"
    DTDFVocab:provides FuelEfficiencyRecommendations
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Forecasting system failures and component degradation using historical and real-time data"
    DTDFVocab:provides FailurePredictionAlerts
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Autonomous decision-making for vehicle adjustments to traffic conditions and environmental factors"
    DTDFVocab:provides AutonomousSpeedAdjustments
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D modeling and real-time dashboards for performance metrics visualization"
    DTDFVocab:provides 3DEnvironmentVisualization
]

instance LifecycleManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "End-to-end tracking of vehicle lifecycle from design to operation and disposal"
    DTDFVocab:provides LifecycleComplianceReports
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance VehiclePerformanceInsights : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring reports of vehicle operational states including speed, diagnostics, and health metrics"
]

instance FuelEfficiencyRecommendations : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated recommendations for optimal speed adjustments to improve fuel efficiency"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FailurePredictionAlerts : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics identifying potential component failures before they occur"
]

instance AutonomousSpeedAdjustments : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Autonomous system adjusting vehicle speed based on real-time traffic and environmental conditions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance 3DEnvironmentVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D representation of vehicle surroundings and object tracks for monitoring"
]

instance LifecycleComplianceReports : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generated reports tracking vehicle lifecycle compliance with safety and regulatory standards"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C1: Environment
instance AutomotiveDigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains VehicleSteeringServo, MotorSpeedController,
                   LiDARUST10LX, IntelDepthCamera,
                   VehicleSpeedSensor, SteeringAngleSensor,
                   MotorActuatorStatus, ServoStatusSensor,
                   PointCloudClusteringComponent,
                   DataTranslationComponent, ROS2Network,
                   WiFiNetwork, DataPreProcessingFE,
                   DataCollectionFE, PresentationFE,
                   DataStorageGenerationFE, UserInterfaceFE,
                   3DVisualizationEngine,
                   VehicleStateModel, EnvironmentalMappingModel,
                   MonitoringService, OptimizationService,
                   PredictiveMaintenanceService, ControlService,
                   VisualizationService, LifecycleManagementService,
                   DataCollectionDatabase, RealTimeDashboards
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
    The **virtual-to-physical interaction** enables the digital twin to command and actuate the physical vehicle, primarily through **Automatic Cruise Control (ACC)** and user-driven controls.

    **Command Types and Message Formats**:
    - The system supports **ACC control commands**, including:
      - Enabling/disabling ACC functionality.
      - Modifying desired speed (e.g., setpoint adjustments).
      - Initiating emergency braking (e.g., collision avoidance).
    - User commands (e.g., manual steering adjustments) are translated into **control signals** (e.g., motor actuation, servo commands) via the **Actuation FE** module.

    **Validation and Authentication**:
    - **Data Translation component** ensures command messages are correctly formatted and validated for physical actuation (e.g., unit consistency checks).
    - **Control Panel FE** (implied in Table I) likely includes validation logic to prevent invalid commands (e.g., speed beyond vehicle limits).

    **Feedback Systems and Confirmation**:
    - The system **sends feedback signals** (e.g., desired speed, braking commands) back to the vehicle’s **Actuation FE** for execution.
    - Actuation confirmation is implied by the requirement that the DTS **actuates the ego vehicle in response to user commands and ACC control** (per Requirement R6).
    - **Conflict resolution** may occur if commands conflict (e.g., manual steering vs. ACC speed setpoint), though specifics are not detailed in the documents.

    **Execution and Synchronization**:
    - Commands are executed via **motor/servo actuation FE** modules, which translate digital signals into physical movements (e.g., throttle, brake, steering adjustments).
    - The **ROS2 network** ensures low-latency synchronization between digital commands and physical actuation (critical for safety-critical functions like braking).
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **time synchronization** with a **100 ms accuracy requirement** for coordination between physical and digital components, critical for computation offloading and safety-critical vehicular applications. This synchronization is enforced via hardware limitations and mandates stricter clock synchronization standards than 100 ms, ensuring real-time alignment for critical operations (e.g., sensor-actuator feedback loops, real-time data processing). The system does not specify explicit synchronization protocols (e.g., NTP, PTP), but the **100 ms latency threshold** is explicitly tied to hardware constraints and functional requirements."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows an **entity-based architecture** derived from ISO 23247 RA, structured into three primary entities:
  1. **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts compute, sensors, actuators, and general automotive functionality (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*), deployed on hardware components (e.g., embedded systems). This entity diverges from ISO 23247 by encompassing broader automotive resources beyond the OME (Operations Management Entity).
  2. **Digital Twin Entity**: Manages data preprocessing, actuation, and control mechanisms, abstracting physical twin data into a digital representation.
  3. **User Entity**: Contains the *User Interface FE*, split into *3D Visualization* and *Control Panel* components, executed as ROS2 packages. The system does not explicitly define orchestration patterns (e.g., microservices, orchestration frameworks like Kubernetes) but relies on **ROS2’s modularity** for component deployment and interoperability. The absence of explicit orchestration mechanisms implies a **loosely coupled** design, where entity boundaries are enforced via hardware deployment and ROS2’s publish-subscribe model."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system is designed with **real-time and high-fidelity** requirements, adhering to **ISO 23247** principles. Key fidelity and validity considerations include:

- **Model Fidelity Requirements**:
  - The Digital Twin Entity must **maintain network latency ≤ 100 ms** (R7) to ensure temporal accuracy.
  - **Clock synchronization** (R8) is enforced across components to preserve data integrity, using **NTP (Network Time Protocol)** or ROS2’s distributed time synchronization.

- **Validation and Verification Methods**:
  - **Real-time validation** is achieved via **ROS2’s message passing** and **sensor fusion algorithms** (e.g., point cloud clustering) to detect discrepancies between physical and digital states.
  - **Uncertainty quantification** is implicit in the system’s reliance on **high-precision sensors** (e.g., LiDAR/Depth Camera) and **error-correction FEs** (e.g., data filtering and alignment).

- **Error Detection and Correction**:
  - The **Actuation FE** monitors actuator performance and triggers corrections if deviations exceed predefined thresholds.
  - **Data integrity checks** (e.g., checksums, ROS2’s reliability guarantees) prevent corrupted inputs from propagating to the Digital Twin Entity.

- **Quality Assurance Processes**:
  - The system’s **ISO 23247 compliance** ensures modularity and interoperability, with each Functional Entity (FE) validated independently for accuracy.
  - **Historical data storage** (SQL databases) enables post-processing validation, though real-time fidelity is prioritized.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
    The **Digital Twin’s technical connection** relies on a hybrid communication architecture combining ROS2-based internal messaging and Wi-Fi-based inter-entity communication.

    **Communication Protocols and Topology**:
    - **Internal Software Components**: Communicate via **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)** over a **ROS2 network**, enabling real-time message passing with standardized serialization (e.g., ROS messages) and type alignment.
    - **Inter-Entity Communication**: Uses **Wi-Fi** (UDP transport layer implied, though exact protocol truncated in documents) for data exchange between the vehicle and external systems (e.g., cloud/edge platforms).
    - **Latency Requirements**: The system must maintain **network latency of ≤100 ms** for critical functions (e.g., ACC control, collision avoidance).

    **Bandwidth and Reliability**:
    - **Bandwidth**: Not explicitly quantified, but ROS2 and UDP are designed for high-throughput, low-latency applications (e.g., sensor data streams).
    - **Reliability**: ROS2 ensures message reliability via **publish-subscribe semantics** and acknowledgments, while Wi-Fi may rely on **UDP’s inherent retransmission mechanisms** (though not explicitly detailed; no redundant links or redundancy protocols specified).

    **Security Protocols**:
    - Security details are **not in Document** (e.g., encryption, authentication for Wi-Fi/ROS2 communication).
    - The architecture does not mention **centralized ROS2 hubs** or distributed topologies; the ROS2 network appears as a **decentralized, component-based system** (per Table I).
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
    The **Digital Twin System (DTS)** employs a **hybrid deployment model** combining **edge computing** and **cloud offloading**, optimized for real-time and non-real-time tasks.

    **Hosting Platforms and Requirements**:
    - **Edge Computing**: Deployed on **NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano microcomputer** for real-time processing (e.g., LiDAR clustering, motor/servo actuation FE).
    - **Cloud Offloading**: Non-real-time tasks (e.g., data storage, reporting) are offloaded to **external storage devices** (e.g., external drives) or cloud platforms.
    - **Computational Requirements**:
      - Edge node: High-performance processing for sensor fusion (e.g., LiDAR point cloud processing).
      - Cloud/storage: Scalable storage for historical vehicle state data (e.g., speed logs, braking events).
    - **Storage Requirements**: External storage must handle **ego vehicle state data** (e.g., telemetry logs) beyond real-time processing.

    **Scalability and Deployment Models**:
    - **Scalability**: Not explicitly detailed, but ROS2’s publish-subscribe model supports horizontal scaling for additional sensors/actuators.
    - **Deployment Models**:
      - **On-vehicle edge deployment** for real-time control (e.g., ACC, steering).
      - **Off-vehicle cloud/edge deployment** for non-critical tasks (e.g., data storage, reporting).
    - **Availability/Reliability**:
      - Edge deployment ensures **low-latency, fault-tolerant** operation for safety-critical functions.
      - Cloud offloading provides redundancy for non-real-time data (e.g., historical reports).
    - **No explicit mention of redundant Wi-Fi links, distributed databases, or horizontal scaling mechanisms** beyond ROS2’s inherent capabilities.
  "
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
  **Standardization:**
  The provided document references the **ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing** standard (2021), which is the most relevant industry standard for Digital Twins in manufacturing. The standard is structured into four parts:

  1. **Overview and General Principles**: Establishes foundational concepts and principles for Digital Twin development.
  2. **Reference Architecture (RA)**:
     - Defines an **entity-based architecture** where each entity (e.g., Observable Manufacturing Element (OME), Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, User Entity) implements specific functionalities.
     - **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**: Represents physical entities like equipment, facilities, products, personnel, or processes.
     - **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**: Manages data acquisition and control of OMEs.
     - **Digital Twin Entity**: Provides digital representation of OMEs.
     - **User Entity**: Offers interfaces for user interaction (e.g., monitoring, control).
     - **Functional Entities (FEs)**: Smaller units within entities responsible for tasks like actuation, data transformation, or user interfacing.
     - **Implementation Flexibility**: The standard does not prescribe specific implementation methods for FEs (e.g., OPC Unified Architecture can be used).

  3. **Digital Representation of Physical Manufacturing Elements**: Not explicitly detailed in the provided text but implied to be part of the framework.
  4. **Information Exchange**: Not explicitly detailed in the provided text but implied to be part of the framework.

  **Key Compliance and Interoperability**:
  - The standard is **ISO-certified**, ensuring alignment with global manufacturing practices.
  - While the document does not specify certification requirements or compliance frameworks beyond ISO 23247, it highlights the **reference architecture’s modularity** and **entity-based design**, which support interoperability and scalability.
  - The standard’s modularity allows integration with other systems (e.g., OPC UA) for data exchange and control.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin System (DTS) must adhere to **ISO 15622:2018** for **adaptive cruise control (ACC)** performance requirements, ensuring compliance with **latency constraints** (≤100 ms) between the vehicle and the Digital Twin Entity as specified in **3GPP TS 22.185 version 15.0.0 (Release 15)**. While no explicit mention of **cybersecurity protocols** or **access control mechanisms** is provided, the system must respect the **safety-critical nature** of the underlying vehicle functions, implicitly aligning with **ISO 26262-1:2018** principles for functional safety in road vehicles. The document does not detail **fail-safe mechanisms**, **threat mitigation strategies**, or **risk assessment approaches**, but the system must operate within the context of **vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communication standards** to ensure reliable, real-time synchronization between physical and digital representations."
]
